name: Irish Stew (2) [Biddy Whyte Lennon]
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the onions and slice them into rounds.
2. Peel the potatoes as thinly as possible. Leave them whole unless they are very large.
3. Cut the meat into good-sized pieces. Small chops can be left whole, larger ones divided in two.
4. Place a layer of onions on the bottom of a heavy casserole, and the meat on top of them.
5. Sprinkle chopped thyme and parsley generously, and season well.
6. Layer the rest of the onions with the potatoes. Sprinkle thyme and parsley again at the last.
7. Add water as needed to achieve the desired consistency in your pot and lid seal. No more than 2 cups is necessary; I typically use about one cup.
8. Bring the mixture to a boil, cover tightly, and place in a preheated oven at 300°F for 2 1/2 - 3 hours.
9. Check towards the end of cooking time and adjust the gravy by adding a little water if it seems too dry. A good stew should have some gravy but not be overly wet.
10. Serve very hot with additional fresh chopped parsley sprinkled on top. Pair with white soda bread to mop up the gravy.
